items.title: Governments warned about $240m Nyrstar subsidy payouts

children.content: A group of international shareholders are warning state and federal governments against giving global commodities giant Trafigura hundreds of millions of dollars to keep smelting operations going in Australia.

items.title: Explosion in feral pig numbers attributed to major flooding in far north SA

children.content: Feral pig numbers are exploding in outback South Australia after record-breaking floods, prompting a call from station owners and the Invasive Species Council for governments to take action.

image.altText: A mob of feral pigs wading across and out of flood waters

items.title: MP calls for investigation into anti-abortion campaigner

children.content: Anti-abortion campaigner Joanna Howe has been called out for potentially breaching campaign financing laws, with one MP labelling her "attack" style as harmful and dangerous.

image.altText: Joanna Howe stands next to women holding up ban feticide sign.

items.title: School worker sexually assaulted girls in Adelaide Hills, court told

children.content: A contract worker accused  of indecently assaulted two students at an Adelaide Hills school he worked at has appeared in court for the first time.

image.altText: A sign that says "Mount Barker Courthouse" stands outside a brick building.

items.title: Arsonist with 'fixated discontent' jailed over $1.5m hotel fire

children.content: A judge has jailed a man who "embarked on a consistent campaign of terror" against his former business partner, including torching the complex containing the Oxford Hotel in North Adelaide.

image.altText: old hotel on fire

items.title: Major milk company fined for misleading consumers about fresh milk

children.content: One of Australia's largest milk providers has been fined by the consumer watchdog for misleading consumers with "fresh milk" labels on products that contained substantial amounts of powdered milk.

image.altText: Milk pouring into a cup with soft green background

items.title: Wettest year on record sees controversial and highly flammable weed 'explode'

children.content: As buffel grass explodes across Central Australia due to high rainfall, there are fears it will spread further east and threaten landmarks in South Australia and other states.

image.altText: A woman holding a large bunch of grass.

items.title: Fans hope to see Thunderbirds win netball grand final for third time

children.content: Among the devoted Thunderbirds fans making the journey to Melbourne for the grand final against the Vixens are the Rawlins family, including sisters Tess and Eve.

avatar.alt: A headshot of a smiling woman with a dark background

items.title: Adelaide gets 70pc of July rain in one day with windy weather to continue

children.content: The Bureau of Meteorology says metropolitan Adelaide has received 70 per cent of its median rainfall for July in a single day, as South Australian farmers welcome the conditions.

image.altText: water overflow from a grass patch over a short wall to a busy road

items.title: 12yo boy allegedly with machete arrested at Adelaide shopping strip

children.content: A 12-year-old boy who allegedly had a machete concealed in his pants was among four boys arrested in Rundle Mall over two days.

image.altText: A black machete case and machete on a table

items.title: Why Alice Springs has been dubbed the lesbian capital of Australia

children.content: New research places Alice Springs in the top 10 most inclusive Australian cities for LGBTQIA+ people, cementing the outback town's long-held reputation as the lesbian capital of the country.

items.title: As main street shops vanish, could they help solve housing shortages?

children.content: As businesses shut up shop in regional main streets, the government is weighing up whether they could help solve the housing crisis.

image.altText: A for-lease sign on the left in an image showing the footpath of  regional shopping street.

items.title: Volunteers say National Trust of SA warned them against speaking publicly

children.content: Volunteers who spoke to the media say they felt "threatened" with formal warnings from the National Trust of South Australia. They are now preparing to share their experiences in a parliamentary inquiry.

image.altText: A woman with cropped white hair and a necklace in a living room

items.title: 'Night of horror' in Kyiv as at least 25 die in Russian attacks across Ukraine

children.content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy cut his Dublin trip short as the strikes trapped paramedics and drivers at an ambulance station.

image.altText: A building is damaged and on fire.

items.title: Childcare safety loophole for educators breaching regulations

children.content: A loophole in the government’s new childcare safety legislation allows educators who have breached safety standards to continue operating.

image.altText: A rubber giraffe-duck, teddy bear and various coloured hoops are seen among other toys.

articles.title: Egypt to give Salah until last minute to prove fitness for Socceroos clash

children.content: The "passionate" Egypt captain trains with the squad but his coach says he won't be rushed into the team to face Australia if he's not fit.

image.altText: Mohamed Salah of Egypt reacts during the training session

items.title: Parents warned against pulling kids out of sport over concussion anxiety

children.content: A research paper published in June found almost a third of parents surveyed said they stopped their children from playing contact sports out of fear of concussion.

items.title: Worst start to ski season since 1957, as blizzard finally dumps snow

children.content: Following the worst start to a ski season in decades, a major snowstorm hit the NSW and Victorian Alps last night.

items.title: Serial harassers given '19 strikes' on Meta, eSafety boss says

children.content: Elon Musk's X Corp is fighting the eSafety Commissioner on three different take-down orders of violent content, including the killing of Charlie Kirk.

items.title: Teenager charged with murder after death in Melbourne's north

children.content: A 16-year-old boy has been charged with murder after a boy was found with stab wounds outside a Craigieburn hospital.

items.title: Why would Taylor Swift get married at Madison Square Garden?

children.content: Fans believe they know the when and where of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's upcoming nuptials, but much of the reported two-day extravaganza will likely be a cloak-and-dagger operation.

items.title: Former Olympian charged over alleged 'vandalism' of US Reflecting Pool

children.content: Former US Olympian David 'Davey' Hearn has been accused of "maliciously" breaking or destroying lining material on the bottom of the Washington DC pool, court documents show

items.title: ASX set to rise after Dow record

children.content: The ASX is expected to open higher after weaker US jobs data eased interest rate hike expectations. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: Sprinter Lachie Kennedy gets his wish ahead of Commonwealth Games debut

children.content: Lachie Kennedy is a big fish in a relatively small pond, but the next few weeks will pit him against some of the world's best as he continues his rise in global athletics.

image.altText: Lachie Kennedy runs out past pyrotechnics at the Maurie Plant Meet.

items.title: Victoria Park closure cuts international students' access to vital refuge

children.content: Foreign university students say the closure of the park to make way for Brisbane Stadium leaves them without a crucial meeting place to help stave off loneliness and isolation.

image.altText: A woman wearing a headscarf looks at the camera while standing in front of a blue industrial fence at a building site.

items.title: Aboriginal activist takes on logging company staff during trespass court case

children.content: An Aboriginal activist has interrogated forestry company staff over their knowledge of cultural heritage as part of her defence against a trespass charge.

image.altText: Ruth Langford, wearing a white jacket, standing in front of the Hobart Magistrates Court smiling

items.title: Vatican excommunicates members of right-wing breakaway group SSPX

children.content: The Vatican has excommunicated priests and lay Catholics who are part of a breakaway right-wing Catholic group that ordained bishops without Pope Leo's approval.

image.altText: Pope Leo in gold and white robes holding up a golden goblet, over a stone platform with other silver goblets.

items.title: Husband welcomes drug-driving law change after crash that claimed wife

children.content: A man devastated by a drug-driving "loophole" that allowed a meth-affected driver to avoid a drug conviction after a fatal crash welcomes new legislation.
